nodes.context: Collective of organized crime elements originating and operating in Russia.
A specific organized criminal group within the Russian mafia.
Russian organized crime group from Solntsevo District in Moscow.
Russian organized crime group from Tambov Oblast.
Russian organized crime group from Izmaylovo District.
Russian organized crime group that supported political candidates.
Collective of organized crime elements originating and operating in Russia.
Italian-American organized crime syndicate operating in the United States.
Colombian drug trafficking organizations.
Gang led by Monya Elson operating from Russia to Los Angeles to New York.
Italian organized crime syndicate based in Campania.
Russian organized crime group based in Moscow.
Russian organized crime group led by Sergei Timofeyev.
Russian mafia clan based in New York.
Russian organized crime clan.
Russian organized crime organization involved in money laundering in Spain.
Russian organized crime organization involved in money laundering in Spain.
Collective of organized crime elements originating and operating in Russia.
Criminal gang based in Saint Petersburg, led by Vladimir Kumarin.
Russia's largest criminal group with about 5,000 members, named after Solntsevo District, led by Sergei Mikhailov.
Moscow-based gang founded by Sergei Timofeyev, reached height in 1990s.
One of Russia's oldest modern gangs, started in mid- to late-1980s, based in Izmaylovo District.
Notorious gang active in Podolsk region and Moscow suburbs in 1990s, led by Sergey Lalakin.
Large criminal group with around 3,000 members, based in Lyubertsy district of Moscow.
Gang originally from the city of Dolgoprudny.
Controls St. Petersburg-Vyborg highway and trucking businesses, involved in theft, robbery, protection racketeering.
Led by Vladimir Putyrsky and Leonid Usvyatsov, organizes cocaine supply and counterfeit dollar trade.
Strong and violent criminal group based in Ryazan city in the 1990s.
Influential organized crime group in Yekaterinburg in the 1990s, founded by Sergei Ivannikov.
One of the largest ethnic organized crime groups in the former Soviet Union.
One of the biggest, powerful and influential criminal networks in Europe.
Paramilitary group involved in organised crime led by Jaba Ioseliani in Georgia in the 1990s.
Multi-ethnic transnational group headed by Temuri Mirzoyev, composed of leaders of several Eurasian criminal groups.
Based in Budapest, Hungary, headed by Semion Mogilevich, numbered approximately 250 members as of 1996.
Criminal organization associated with Vyacheslav Ivankov.
Colombia-based cartel that supplied cocaine to the Ismailovskaya mafia and the Uzbek criminal network.
Japanese organized crime groups from Kobe and Osaka, aligned with Tambov gang.
Caucasian criminal clan led by Aslan Usoyan.
Caucasian criminal clan led by Aslan Usoyan.
Considered the most influential organized crime group in Russia, led by Tariel Oniani.
Moscow criminal group led by Valery Dlugach.
Criminal group from Tolyatti, led by Dmitry Ruzlyaev, involved in Tolyatti crime war.
Gang based in Kushchyovskaya, led by Sergei Tsapkov.

edges.evidence_quote: Any of the mafia's groups may be referred to as an "Organized Criminal Group" (OPG, from Russian "организованная преступная группа"). This is sometimes modified to include a specific name, such as the Orekhovskaya OPG.
edges.context: Orekhovskaya OPG is a specific group within the Russian mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: Moreover, many of these groups are named according to their regional origins, such as the Solntsevskaya Bratva (from the Solntsevo District in Moscow), the Tambovskaya Bratva (from Tambov Oblast), the Izmaylovskaya gang (from the Izmaylovo District), among other.
edges.context: Solntsevskaya Bratva is a group within the Russian mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: Moreover, many of these groups are named according to their regional origins, such as the Solntsevskaya Bratva (from the Solntsevo District in Moscow), the Tambovskaya Bratva (from Tambov Oblast), the Izmaylovskaya gang (from the Izmaylovo District), among other.
edges.context: Tambovskaya Bratva is a group within the Russian mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: Moreover, many of these groups are named according to their regional origins, such as the Solntsevskaya Bratva (from the Solntsevo District in Moscow), the Tambovskaya Bratva (from Tambov Oblast), the Izmaylovskaya gang (from the Izmaylovo District), among other.
edges.context: Izmaylovskaya gang is a group within the Russian mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: The Uralmash gang, for example, openly supported political candidates and formed a public movement to gain legitimacy.
edges.context: Uralmash gang is a group within the Russian mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: Within a year, he built an international operation that included but was not limited to narcotics, money laundering, and prostitution and made ties with the American Mafia and Colombian drug cartels
edges.context: Ivankov established ties between Russian mafia and American Mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: Within a year, he built an international operation that included but was not limited to narcotics, money laundering, and prostitution and made ties with the American Mafia and Colombian drug cartels
edges.context: Ivankov established ties between Russian mafia and Colombian drug cartels.

edges.evidence_quote: In 1995, the Camorra cooperated with the Russian mafia in a scheme in which the Camorra would bleach out US$1 bills and reprint them as $100s.
edges.context: Camorra and Russian mafia cooperated in a counterfeiting scheme.

edges.evidence_quote: Ivankov regularly flew around Europe and Asia to maintain ties with his fellow mobsters (like members of the Solntsevskaya Bratva)
edges.context: Ivankov maintained ties with Solntsevskaya Bratva.

edges.evidence_quote: Ivankov got into another altercation with drug kingpin and head of the Orekhovskaya gang, Segei 'Sylvester' Timofeyev, ending with the latter murdered a month later.
edges.context: Ivankov had a conflict with the Orekhovskaya gang leader, resulting in his murder.

edges.evidence_quote: Semion Mogilevich had alliances with the Camorra, in particular with Salvatore DeFalco, a lower-echelon member of the Giuliano clan.
edges.context: Mogilevich, associated with Russian mafia, had alliances with Camorra.

edges.evidence_quote: It is believed that 27 of the arrested are connected to the Russian mafia Shulaya clan which are largely based in New York.
edges.context: Shulaya clan is a part of the Russian mafia.

edges.evidence_quote: The raids resulted in the arrests of 11 members and associates of the Solntsevskaya and Izmailovskaya clans.
edges.context: Izmailovskaya clan is a Russian mafia clan.

edges.evidence_quote: The Tambov and Malyshev Russian mafia organisations were involved.
edges.context: Tambov mafia is a Russian mafia organization.

edges.evidence_quote: The Tambov and Malyshev Russian mafia organisations were involved.
edges.context: Malyshev mafia is a Russian mafia organization.

edges.evidence_quote: Izmailovskaya has good relations with the Podolskaya gang.
edges.context: The Izmaylovskaya gang has good relations with the Podolskaya gang.

edges.evidence_quote: The Colombia-based Cali KGB Cartel supplied cocaine to the Ismailovskaya mafia, too.
edges.context: The Cali KGB Cartel supplied cocaine to the Izmaylovskaya gang.

edges.evidence_quote: Komarovskaya OPG steal automobiles, commit robberies, provide protection racketeering, and receive strong support from the Usvyatsov-Putyrsky gang and its AOZT "Putus" to organize the supply of cocaine from South America into Russia, Finland, Scandinavia and Europe and the trade in counterfeit dollars.
edges.context: The Komarovskaya OPG receives strong support from the Usvyatsov-Putyrsky gang for cocaine supply and counterfeit dollar trade.

edges.evidence_quote: The Usvyatsov-Putyrsky gang (AOZT "Putus") ... works closely with the Komarovskaya organized criminal group.
edges.context: The Usvyatsov-Putyrsky gang works closely with the Komarovskaya organized criminal group.

edges.evidence_quote: Its business is often connected with that of the Solntsevskaya Bratva and the Vyacheslav Ivankov Organization.
edges.context: The Semion Mogilevich organization's business is often connected with the Solntsevskaya Bratva.

edges.evidence_quote: Its business is often connected with that of the Solntsevskaya Bratva and the Vyacheslav Ivankov Organization.
edges.context: The Semion Mogilevich organization's business is often connected with the Vyacheslav Ivankov Organization.

edges.evidence_quote: The Tambov gang of Saint Petersburg is very closely aligned with ... Japanese yakuza from Kobe and Osaka.
edges.context: The Tambov gang is very closely aligned with Japanese yakuza from Kobe and Osaka.

edges.evidence_quote: It had a long-term bloody wars with other active criminal groups in the city (Ayrapetovskaya, Kochetkovskie, etc.) with which it initially coexisted peacefully.
edges.context: The Slonovskaya gang had long-term bloody wars with the Ayrapetovskaya gang.

edges.evidence_quote: It had a long-term bloody wars with other active criminal groups in the city (Ayrapetovskaya, Kochetkovskie, etc.) with which it initially coexisted peacefully.
edges.context: The Slonovskaya gang had long-term bloody wars with the Kochetkovskie gang.

edges.evidence_quote: The Colombia-based Cali KGB Cartel supplied cocaine to this network, too.
edges.context: The Cali KGB Cartel supplied cocaine to the Uzbek criminal network.
